mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand, which directly translates to higher corporate revenues and earnings, thereby driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: If the 3-month annualized growth rate of RSAFS shows no statistically significant positive correlation with S&P 500 20-day returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in the unemployment rate below a critical threshold signals a tightening labor market, which often precedes wage inflation and potential monetary policy tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are predominantly positive (more than 55% of triggered instances) when UNRATE drops below 7.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of low federal funds rates and increasing housing starts indicates an accommodative monetary environment supporting a recovering real estate sector, which stimulates broader economic activity and corporate profits, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < 0.25% and HOUST 3-month average growth > 5% annualized
failure_condition: If periods characterized by low FEDFUNDS and strong HOUST growth consistently yield negative or flat S&P 500 60-day returns (average return < 0.5%).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of confirmed economic expansion, robust industrial production growth signals strong manufacturing and business investment, which directly boosts corporate earnings and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If INDPRO growth fails to predict positive S&P 500 returns during confirmed periods of economic expansion, or if the correlation turns negative over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating inflation, as indicated by CPIAUCSL exceeding a specific annualized growth threshold, signals potential erosion of purchasing power and corporate margins, likely prompting central bank tightening, which negatively impacts equity valuations and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are predominantly positive (more than 50% of triggered instances) when CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change exceeds 3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent positive growth in non-farm payrolls indicates a healthy and expanding labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate revenues, translating into positive S&P 500 forward returns over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: If the 1-month growth rate of PAYEMS shows no statistically significant positive correlation with S&P 500 5-day returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of strong GDP growth and declining unemployment signals a robust and accelerating economy, which boosts investor confidence and corporate earnings expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth > 2.5% and UNRATE 3-month change < 0
failure_condition: If periods of high GDP growth and falling unemployment consistently result in negative or flat S&P 500 20-day returns (average return < 0.2%).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of low and stable inflation, a stable federal funds rate indicates a predictable and non-disruptive monetary policy environment, reducing uncertainty and supporting equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day returns are consistently negative or flat when FEDFUNDS is stable and CPIAUCSL is below 2.0% (average return < 0.1%).
